description_long: This is a double-bladed axe with a broad, symmetrical head featuring two large, angular cutting edges extending from either side of a central mounting block. The blades are metallic and have a matte, slightly rough finish, with a geometric, almost polygonal outline rather than a traditional curved edge. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood, wrapped with a reddish-brown grip section for improved handling. The transition between the handle and the blade is reinforced with a blocky, beige collar, suggesting added durability. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding, and the proportions suggest it is intended for two-handed use. The axe is robust and functional, suitable for heavy-duty tasks in an urban or industrial context.
description: A double-bladed, polygonal axe with a straight wooden handle and a broad, matte metallic head. The design is utilitarian and robust, with a reddish-brown grip and no visible decorations.
description_view_0: The axe is seen at an angle, highlighting the thickness of the double blades and the reddish-brown grip on the handle.
description_view_1: A direct side view shows the full width and symmetry of the double blades, as well as the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: This angled view emphasizes the blade's edge profile and the blocky collar where the handle meets the head.
description_view_3: A top-down view reveals the polygonal, symmetrical shape of the blades and the central mounting block.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a reddish-brown grip wrap, no curvature, round cross-section, utilitarian style.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bladed, broad, polygonal edges with symmetrical design, no beard or poll, matte finish, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a blocky collar securing the blade to the handle.
